findings.title: Pipeline URMA write result recording is untested
findings.impact: The new failover accounting records pipeline URMA transfer success or failure, but the added ST traffic uses 512 KiB values while the default URMA max write size is 2 MiB, so it exercises the single-write path rather than this pipeline branch. A regression where large-object pipeline failures are not recorded would leave clients pinned to a bad URMA data plane for large payloads.
findings.fix: Add a URMA failover test that forces the pipeline path, for example by writing an object/value larger than the configured URMA max write size or lowering the test max-write-size flag, inject a pipeline UrmaWritePayload failure, and assert that the unhealthy window triggers the same worker switch.

findings.title: New URMA failover gflag validators lack negative tests
findings.impact: The PR adds validation for NaN, out-of-range success-rate ratios, and zero min-sample counts, but the new tests only cover valid runtime values. Invalid config updates could be accepted, rejected incorrectly, or leave stale values without any regression signal.
findings.fix: Add focused validator or client-config-monitor tests that attempt invalid values such as -0.1, 1.1, NaN/inf, and min_sample_count=0, then assert the update is rejected and the previous valid configuration remains in effect.
